Cookbook | Recipes | Ingredients | Equipment | Techniques | Cookbook Disambiguation Pages | Ingredients  | Spices and herbs

**Green cardamom** or **elaichi** is a pungent aromatic spice. It is separate from black cardamom, which has different characteristics.

## Characteristics

[edit | edit source]

Cardamom takes the form of a small green pod containing several small dark seeds.[[1]](#cite_note-:42-1) The immature pods are harvested and dried,[[1]](#cite_note-:42-1)[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) and some forms of processing may treat them with a bleaching step to make the pods white[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)—this may slightly reduce the aroma, but it is not a dealbreaker.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

Overall, cardamom has a quite strong sweet flavor, with hints of eucalyptus from the cineole it contains.[[1]](#cite_note-:42-1)[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3) It also has other trace compounds that give it its unique profile.[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)

## Selection and storage

[edit | edit source]

Whole pods or seeds are preferred, as the aroma and flavor are preserved for longer—grind as close to use as possible.[[4]](#cite_note-:2-4) If purchasing seeds, they should be dark in color and just a little sticky.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) As with all spices, keep cardamom in an airtight container at ambient temperature, away from light, heat, and moisture.

## Use

[edit | edit source]

Cardamom is used in a variety of cuisines, both sweet and savory, especially across Asia and in northern Europe.[[4]](#cite_note-:2-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) It often features in spice blends, such as curry powders and garam masala, and can be mixed with tea or coffee.[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Because the majority of its flavor compounds are fat-soluble, heating cardamom seeds in oil is an excellent way to ensure their flavor is spread through the entire dish.[[4]](#cite_note-:2-4) Whole crushed cardamom pods can be used,*[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)* though this is best in dishes where they can be easily identified and removed to avoid their fibrous texture.[[4]](#cite_note-:2-4)

## Substitution
